title: Professional Tax Registration — Bangalore
description: Professional Tax Registration — Bangalore online by CA firm. Expert filing and compliance support. Virtual Auditor.
bodyText:
Professional Tax (PT) in Karnataka is a direct tax on all persons engaged in profession, trade, or employment. Employer registration mandatory if employing persons in Karnataka. Employee deduction: up to ₹2,500/year (based on salary slab). Monthly return filing required. Virtual Auditor's Bangalore office at MG Road handles PT registration and compliance. Quick Answer: Professional Tax Registration — Bangalore — Professional Tax Registration — Bangalore online by CA firm. Expert filing and compliance support. Virtual Auditor.

HEADING2: Professional Tax Rates in Karnataka (2024-25)

Karnataka levies professional tax under the Karnataka Tax on Professions, Trades, Callings and Employments Act, 1976. The tax applies to every person engaged in any profession, trade, calling or employment in the state.

Monthly Salary Range | Monthly PT Amount | Annual PT |

Up to ₹15,000 | Nil | Nil |

₹15,001 to ₹25,000 | ₹200 | ₹2,400 |

Above ₹25,000 | ₹200 | ₹2,500 (max) |

Employer obligation: Every employer in Karnataka must obtain PT Registration Certificate (PTRC) and deduct PT from employee salaries monthly. The employer must also pay ₹2,500 per year for the business establishment itself (PTEC). Returns must be filed monthly by the 20th of the following month.

Penalty for non-compliance: Interest at 1.25% per month on delayed payment. Penalty up to ₹1,000 per month for non-registration. The Commercial Tax Department can recover arrears as land revenue.

Virtual Auditor's Bangalore office at Mahalakshmi Chambers, MG Road handles PT registration, monthly return filing, and compliance management as part of our payroll service package.

Source: Karnataka Tax on Professions, Trades, Callings and Employments Act, 1976. Karnataka Government ↗

HEADING3: Virtual Auditor vs Self-Filing vs Online Aggregators
When it comes to professional tax registration, you have three choices: self-filing through government portals, using an online aggregator, or engaging a qualified CA firm like Virtual Auditor. Self-filing saves fees but risks errors that trigger notices and penalties. Online aggregators offer low-cost templated services but lack the expertise to handle complications. Virtual Auditor provides practitioner-level expertise with personalised attention — every engagement is supervised by CA V. Viswanathan (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV), ensuring accuracy, regulatory compliance, and strategic advisory that goes beyond mere filing.
HEADING3: What You Will Receive
Upon completion, you will receive: Professional Tax Registration Certificate (PTRC and PTEC) from Karnataka Commercial Taxes Department, monthly/annual PT return filing schedule, salary slab-wise PT deduction chart for Karnataka, PT challan template and payment process documentation, and compliance calendar covering filing deadlines. Karnataka PT rates: up to ₹200/month per employee depending on salary slab.
HEADING3: Latest Regulatory Updates (FY 2025-26)
This page has been updated to reflect changes introduced in Budget 2025, recent notifications from CBDT, CBIC, MCA, SEBI, and RBI, and evolving compliance requirements for FY 2025-26. Virtual Auditor continuously monitors regulatory developments to ensure all advice and filings are current and compliant with the latest provisions.
HEADING2: Recent Engagement — How We Helped
Context: a group of 4 co-founders launching an AI-powered fintech startup in Bangalore.
Challenge: The founders needed to incorporate quickly to sign a term sheet with an angel investor, but had complex requirements — one NRI director, customised Articles of Association with vesting clauses, and simultaneous DPIIT startup recognition for tax benefits.
Our approach: We handled end-to-end incorporation using SPICe+ (INC-32), securing DSC for all 4 directors including the NRI (using foreign address attestation), drafted customised MOA/AOA with founder vesting and anti-dilution provisions, and filed DPIIT recognition immediately post-incorporation.
Outcome: Certificate of Incorporation received in 6 working days. PAN/TAN/GST registration allotted simultaneously through SPICe+. DPIIT recognition approved within 48 hours of incorporation. The angel round closed within 3 weeks of engagement.
This engagement illustrates Virtual Auditor's approach to professional tax registration — combining regulatory expertise with practical execution to deliver results within the client's timeline.
HEADING2: When Is Professional Tax Registration Not Required?
Professional Tax registration may not be required when: (a) all employees/professionals earn below the Karnataka PT threshold (currently Rs 15,000/month), (b) the business has no employees (sole proprietor with no staff), (c) the entity is exempt under the Karnataka Tax on Professions Act, or (d) PT is already being deducted by the employer. Employers with salaried employees in Karnataka must register for PT deduction.
If you are unsure whether your situation requires professional tax registration, contact us for a free preliminary assessment. We will advise you honestly — including telling you if you do not need our services.
HEADING2: Timeline and Turnaround
Typical turnaround for professional tax registration: 5-15 working days from submission of complete documents. Name approval: 1-3 working days. SPICe+ incorporation: 3-6 working days after name approval. LLP incorporation: 5-8 working days. Complex registrations (Section 8 company, Nidhi, Producer Company): 10-15 working days due to additional approvals required.
Timelines assume prompt submission of complete documents and information. We provide a clear project timeline at the start of every engagement.
HEADING2: Who Needs Professional Tax Registration?
This registration is required for: (a) businesses seeking limited liability protection for promoters and directors, (b) startups planning to raise equity funding from investors (angel/VC/PE), (c) entities requiring a separate legal identity for contracts, property, and bank accounts, (d) businesses planning to scale operations across multiple states, (e) professionals or consultants seeking to formalise their practice into a body corporate, and (f) any person or group mandated by law to register under the applicable business structure.
HEADING3: Why Choose a CA Firm Over Self-Filing or Aggregators?
Self-filing through government portals is an option for straightforward cases, but it carries risks — incorrect classification, missed deductions, or procedural errors that can trigger notices and penalties. Online aggregators offer low prices but use templated approaches without understanding your specific regulatory situation. Virtual Auditor provides practitioner-grade expertise: every engagement is handled by qualified professionals (FCA, ACS, CFE, IBBI RV) who understand the regulatory nuances, anticipate complications, and provide ongoing support beyond the filing.

faqs.q: What is the PT rate in Karnataka?
faqs.a: Employees: ₹200/month (salary >₹15,000). Self-employed: ₹2,500/year. Rates specified in Karnataka Tax on Professions, Trades, Callings and Employments Act, 1976.

faqs.q: What is professional tax in Karnataka?
faqs.a: Direct tax on persons in profession, trade, or employment. Maximum Rs 2,500/year. Employer registration (PTRC) mandatory.

faqs.q: What are PT slab rates in Karnataka?
faqs.a: Up to Rs 15,000/month: Nil. Rs 15,001-25,000: Rs 200/month. Above Rs 25,000: Rs 200/month (max Rs 2,500/year).

faqs.q: What is the due date for PT?
faqs.a: Monthly by 20th of following month. Late payment: 1.25% interest/month. Non-registration penalty: Rs 1,000/month.

faqs.q: Is PT deductible from income tax?
faqs.a: Yes. Deductible under Section 16(iii) of Income Tax Act from gross salary income. Employer claims as business expenditure.

faqs.q: Does PT apply to company directors?
faqs.a: Directors drawing salary within PT slab are covered. Non-executive directors with only sitting fees are generally not covered.
